# Statistical differences
hist(total_MUFA_per_snail $ sum_MUFA) 

shapiro.test(total_MUFA_per_snail $ sum_MUFA) 

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  total_MUFA_per_snail$sum_MUFA
W = 0.90312, p-value = 0.002714
leveneTest(sum_MUFA ~ treatment, data = total_MUFA_per_snail)
Warning in leveneTest.default(y = y, group = group, ...): group coerced to
factor.
Levene's Test for Homogeneity of Variance (center = median)
      Df F value  Pr(>F)  
group  3  3.6404 0.02192 *
      35                  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
pairwise.wilcox.test(total_MUFA_per_snail$sum_MUFA,
                     total_MUFA_per_snail$treatment,
                     p.adjust.method = "BH")

    Pairwise comparisons using Wilcoxon rank sum exact test 

data:  total_MUFA_per_snail$sum_MUFA and total_MUFA_per_snail$treatment 

    Cip    Mix    NC    
Mix 0.0044 -      -     
NC  0.7394 0.0044 -     
Pro 0.5230 0.5230 0.4351

P value adjustment method: BH 
# Calculate mean and 95% CI per treatment
mean_MUFA_sa <- total_MUFA_per_snail %>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_MUFA = mean(sum_MUFA, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(sum_MUFA)),
    se = sd(sum_MUFA, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_MUFA - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_MUFA + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_MUFA, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

# Extract mean of control group for percental difference
control_mean_MUFA_sa <- mean_MUFA_sa %>%
  filter(treatment == "NC") %>%
  pull(mean_MUFA)

# Calculate percental difference from control
mean_MUFA_sa <- mean_MUFA_sa %>%
  mutate(percent_diff_from_NC = 100 * (mean_MUFA - control_mean_MUFA_sa) / control_mean_MUFA_sa)

print(mean_MUFA_sa)
# A tibble: 4 × 6
  treatment mean_MUFA ci_lower ci_upper    se percent_diff_from_NC
  <chr>         <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>                <dbl>
1 Cip           143.     127.      158.  6.79                -2.41
2 Mix            77.1     47.5     107. 12.8                -47.3 
3 NC            146.     130.      162.  7.07                 0   
4 Pro           108.      65.8     150. 18.7                -26.1

3) Total PUFA content

# filter poly-unsaturated fatty acids in new table
PUFA_sa <- FA_Snails_absolute %>%
  filter(fa %in% c("C18:2(n-6c)", "C18:3(n-3)", "C18:3(n-6)", "C18:4(n-3)", "C20:2(n-6)", "C20:3(n-6)", "C20:4(n-6)", "C20:5(n-3)", "C22:5(n-3)", "C22:6(n-3)"))
  
# Sum PUFA per replicate and treatment
total_PUFA_per_snail <- PUFA_sa %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(replicate, treatment) %>% 
  dplyr::summarize(sum_PUFA = sum(fa_conc), .groups = "drop") 
print(total_PUFA_per_snail) 
# A tibble: 39 × 3
   replicate treatment sum_PUFA
   <chr>     <chr>        <dbl>
 1 S_1       NC           117. 
 2 S_10      NC            57.8
 3 S_11      Pro           68.5
 4 S_12      Pro           62.2
 5 S_13      Pro           39.1
 6 S_14      Pro           45.1
 7 S_15      Pro           44.7
 8 S_16      Pro           51.1
 9 S_17      Pro           68.3
10 S_18      Pro          106. 
# ℹ 29 more rows
# statistics
hist(total_PUFA_per_snail $ sum_PUFA)

shapiro.test(total_PUFA_per_snail $ sum_PUFA) 

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  total_PUFA_per_snail$sum_PUFA
W = 0.96225, p-value = 0.2118
leveneTest(sum_PUFA ~ treatment, data = total_PUFA_per_snail)
Warning in leveneTest.default(y = y, group = group, ...): group coerced to
factor.
Levene's Test for Homogeneity of Variance (center = median)
      Df F value Pr(>F)
group  3  0.5787 0.6329
      35               
anova_PUFA <- aov(sum_PUFA ~ treatment, data = total_PUFA_per_snail)
summary(anova_PUFA)
            Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value   Pr(>F)    
treatment    3   9266  3088.6   7.365 0.000596 ***
Residuals   35  14677   419.3                     
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
TukeyHSD(anova_PUFA)
  Tukey multiple comparisons of means
    95% family-wise confidence level

Fit: aov(formula = sum_PUFA ~ treatment, data = total_PUFA_per_snail)

$treatment
              diff        lwr       upr     p adj
Mix-Cip -20.300466 -45.675259  5.074328 0.1552759
NC-Cip   22.733869  -1.964142 47.431879 0.0803987
Pro-Cip  -6.302387 -31.000397 18.395624 0.9009094
NC-Mix   43.034334  17.659540 68.409128 0.0003238
Pro-Mix  13.998079 -11.376715 39.372872 0.4554697
Pro-NC  -29.036255 -53.734266 -4.338245 0.0159242
# Calculate mean and 95% CI per treatment
mean_PUFA_sa <- total_PUFA_per_snail %>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_PUFA = mean(sum_PUFA, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(sum_PUFA)),
    se = sd(sum_PUFA, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_PUFA - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_PUFA + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_PUFA, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

# Extract mean of control group for percental difference
control_mean_PUFA_sa <- mean_PUFA_sa %>%
  filter(treatment == "NC") %>%
  pull(mean_PUFA)

# Calculate percental difference from control
mean_PUFA_sa <- mean_PUFA_sa %>%
  mutate(percent_diff_from_NC = 100 * (mean_PUFA - control_mean_PUFA_sa) / control_mean_PUFA_sa)

print(mean_PUFA_sa)
# A tibble: 4 × 6
  treatment mean_PUFA ci_lower ci_upper    se percent_diff_from_NC
  <chr>         <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>                <dbl>
1 Cip            72.0     57.3     86.6  6.47                -24.0
2 Mix            51.7     39.7     63.6  5.17                -45.4
3 NC             94.7     78.4    111.   7.22                  0  
4 Pro            65.7     50.1     81.3  6.90                -30.7

4) Total FA per snail

# total FA 
total_FA_snails <- bind_rows(total_SAFA_per_snail, total_MUFA_per_snail, total_PUFA_per_snail)

total_FA_per_snail <- total_FA_snails %>%
  dplyr::group_by(replicate, treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(sum_FA = sum(sum_SAFA, sum_MUFA, sum_PUFA, na.rm = TRUE))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'replicate'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.
print(total_FA_per_snail)
# A tibble: 39 × 3
# Groups:   replicate [39]
   replicate treatment sum_FA
   <chr>     <chr>      <dbl>
 1 S_1       NC          594.
 2 S_10      NC          269.
 3 S_11      Pro         425.
 4 S_12      Pro         266.
 5 S_13      Pro         225.
 6 S_14      Pro         316.
 7 S_15      Pro         186.
 8 S_16      Pro         187.
 9 S_17      Pro         345.
10 S_18      Pro         368.
# ℹ 29 more rows
# statistical differences will be calculated by PERMANOVA

# Calculate mean and 95% CI per treatment
mean_FA_sa <- total_FA_per_snail %>%
  dplyr::group_by(treatment) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_FA = mean(sum_FA, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(sum_FA)),
    se = sd(sum_FA, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
    t_crit = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    ci_lower = mean_FA - t_crit * se,
    ci_upper = mean_FA + t_crit * se,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(treatment, mean_FA, ci_lower, ci_upper, se)

# Extract mean of control group for percental difference
control_mean_FA_sa <- mean_FA_sa %>%
  filter(treatment == "NC") %>%
  pull(mean_FA)

# Calculate percental difference from control
mean_FA_sa <- mean_FA_sa %>%
  mutate(percent_diff_from_NC = 100 * (mean_FA - control_mean_FA_sa) / control_mean_FA_sa)

print(mean_FA_sa)
# A tibble: 4 × 6
  treatment mean_FA ci_lower ci_upper    se percent_diff_from_NC
  <chr>       <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>                <dbl>
1 Cip          430.     389.     470.  17.7                 3.97
2 Mix          269.     208.     329.  26.1               -35.0 
3 NC           413.     353.     473.  26.7                 0   
4 Pro          310.     245.     376.  29.1               -24.9

6.2 Histogram FA snails

hist(FA_Snails_absolute $ fa_conc) 

skewness(FA_Snails_absolute $ fa_conc, na.rm = TRUE) 
[1] 2.738149
shapiro.test(FA_Snails_absolute $ fa_conc)

    Shapiro-Wilk normality test

data:  FA_Snails_absolute$fa_conc
W = 0.54871, p-value < 2.2e-16

Fatty acid concentration not normal distributed and right skewed.

6.3 NMDS FA snails

Change table to wide format:

# change table to wide format (sqrt for square root transformation in next step)
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt <-
  pivot_wider(FA_Snails_absolute,
              names_from = fa,
              values_from = fa_conc)

# square root transformation, to reduce discriminatory power of dominant FA groups
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt[, 3:23] <- sqrt(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt[, 3:23])

# Only keep columns/FAs with more than 5 non-zero values (fatty acids that have just been found in 5 replicates will be removed)
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt <-
  FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt %>%
  dplyr::select(
    1:2,
    where(~sum(. !=0, na.rm = TRUE)>5)
  )

Perform metaMDS:

# Remove first and second column for metaMDS
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt <- FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt[, -c(1, 2)]

# Rename (- C, - () ) fatty acids for later plot
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt <- FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt %>%
  rename(
    `12:0`     = `C12:0`,   
    `13:0`     = `C13:0`,
    `14:0`     = `C14:0`,
    `15:0`     = `C15:0`,
    `16:0`     = `C16:0`,
    `16:1`     = `C16:1`,
    `18:0`     = `C18:0`,
    `18:1`     = `C18:1`,
    `18:2n-6c` = `C18:2(n-6c)`,
    `18:3n-6`  = `C18:3(n-6)`,
    `18:3n-3`  = `C18:3(n-3)`,
    `18:4n-3`  = `C18:4(n-3)`,
    `20:0`     = `C20:0`,
    `20:1`     = `C20:1`,
    `20:2n-6`  = `C20:2(n-6)`,
    `20:3n-6`  = `C20:3(n-6)`,
    `20:4n-6`  = `C20:4(n-6)`,
    `20:5n-3`  = `C20:5(n-3)`,
    `22:1`     = `C22:1`,
    `22:5n-3`  = `C22:5(n-3)`,
    `22:6n-3`  = `C22:6(n-3)`
)


NMDS_Snails_absolute <-
  metaMDS(
    FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt,
    autotransform = FALSE,
    k = 2 ,
    distance = "bray",
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
Run 0 stress 0.165764 
Run 1 stress 0.1551301 
... New best solution
... Procrustes: rmse 0.07415017  max resid 0.2626071 
Run 2 stress 0.1640382 
Run 3 stress 0.1811009 
Run 4 stress 0.1551301 
... New best solution
... Procrustes: rmse 8.502898e-06  max resid 3.495941e-05 
... Similar to previous best
Run 5 stress 0.1870022 
Run 6 stress 0.1640382 
Run 7 stress 0.1551301 
... New best solution
... Procrustes: rmse 1.970835e-06  max resid 6.955154e-06 
... Similar to previous best
Run 8 stress 0.1811009 
Run 9 stress 0.1672588 
Run 10 stress 0.1551301 
... Procrustes: rmse 1.500809e-06  max resid 4.747405e-06 
... Similar to previous best
Run 11 stress 0.1661944 
Run 12 stress 0.1676732 
Run 13 stress 0.1676622 
Run 14 stress 0.1640382 
Run 15 stress 0.1870022 
Run 16 stress 0.1640382 
Run 17 stress 0.1551301 
... Procrustes: rmse 3.055054e-06  max resid 1.233022e-05 
... Similar to previous best
Run 18 stress 0.1640382 
Run 19 stress 0.1551301 
... Procrustes: rmse 2.284616e-06  max resid 8.780147e-06 
... Similar to previous best
Run 20 stress 0.1956249 
*** Best solution repeated 4 times
NMDS_Snails_absolute$stress
[1] 0.1551301

Create fa vectors:

# separation in SAFA; MUFA & PUFA to build vectors in NMDS
# "sa" = snail absolute
FA_variables.SAFAs_sa <-
  envfit(
    NMDS_Snails_absolute,
    subset(
      FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt,
      select = c(
        "12:0",
        "13:0",
        "14:0",
        "15:0",
        "16:0",
        "18:0",
        "20:0"
      )
    )
    ,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

FA_variables.MUFAs_sa <-
  envfit(
    NMDS_Snails_absolute,
    subset(
      FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt,
      select = c(
        "16:1",
        "18:1",
        "20:1",
        "22:1"
      )
    )
    ,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

FA_variables.PUFAs_sa <-
  envfit(
    NMDS_Snails_absolute,
    subset(
      FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt,
      select = c(
        "18:2n-6c",
        "18:3n-6",
        "18:3n-3",
        "18:4n-3",
        "20:2n-6",
        "20:3n-6",
        "20:4n-6",
        "20:5n-3",
        "22:5n-3",
        "22:6n-3"
      )
    )
    ,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )

# Extract NMDS scores 
data.scores_snail_absolute = as.data.frame(scores(NMDS_Snails_absolute)$sites)

Treatments <-
  FA_Snails_absolute $ treatment[FA_Snails_absolute $ fa == "C16:0"]

# add treatment column to NMDS scores
data.scores_snail_absolute$Treatment = Treatments

# vectors for SAFA, MUFA and PUFA
SAFAs_coord_cont_sa = as.data.frame(scores(FA_variables.SAFAs_sa, "vectors")) * ordiArrowMul(FA_variables.SAFAs_sa)
MUFAs_coord_cont_sa = as.data.frame(scores(FA_variables.MUFAs_sa, "vectors")) * ordiArrowMul(FA_variables.MUFAs_sa)
PUFAs_coord_cont_sa = as.data.frame(scores(FA_variables.PUFAs_sa, "vectors")) * ordiArrowMul(FA_variables.PUFAs_sa)

Plot NMDS:

# For the plot: reorder treatment variable in data.scores_snail_absolute
data.scores_snail_absolute$Treatment <- factor(
  data.scores_snail_absolute$Treatment,
  levels = c("NC", "Pro", "Cip", "Mix"),
  labels  = c("Control", "Propyzamide", "Ciprofloxacin", "Mixture")
)

# Compute convex hull for each Treatment
polygon_data_snails <- data.scores_snail_absolute %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(hull_points = list(data.frame(
    NMDS1 = NMDS1[chull(NMDS1, NMDS2)],
    NMDS2 = NMDS2[chull(NMDS1, NMDS2)]
  ))) %>%
  unnest(hull_points)

# Function to rescale coordinates
rescale_vectors <- function(df, scale_factor = 1) {
  df$length <- sqrt(df$NMDS1^2 + df$NMDS2^2)
  max_length <- max(df$length)
  df$NMDS1 <- df$NMDS1 / max_length * scale_factor
  df$NMDS2 <- df$NMDS2 / max_length * scale_factor
  df$length <- NULL
  return(df)
}

# Apply to each group 
PUFAs_coord_cont_sa <- rescale_vectors(PUFAs_coord_cont_sa, 0.4)
MUFAs_coord_cont_sa <- rescale_vectors(MUFAs_coord_cont_sa, 0.4)
SAFAs_coord_cont_sa <- rescale_vectors(SAFAs_coord_cont_sa, 0.4)

# plot
absol_snail_with_vectors <- ggplot(data = data.scores_snail_absolute, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
    geom_polygon(
    data = polygon_data_snails,
    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = Treatment, fill = Treatment),
    alpha = 0.2,
    show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(shape = Treatment, color = Treatment),
    size = 5,
    alpha = 1,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Control" = 16, "Propyzamide" = 17, "Ciprofloxacin" = 18, "Mixture" = 15)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Control" = "#009E73", "Propyzamide" = "#E69F00", "Ciprofloxacin" = "#56B4E9", "Mixture" = "#999999")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Control" = "#009E73", "Propyzamide" = "#E69F00", "Ciprofloxacin" = "#56B4E9", "Mixture" = "#999999")) + 
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = NMDS1, yend = NMDS2), 
               data = PUFAs_coord_cont_sa, linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.2, colour = "black") +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = NMDS1, yend = NMDS2), 
               data = MUFAs_coord_cont_sa, linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.2, colour = "black") +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = NMDS1, yend = NMDS2), 
               data = SAFAs_coord_cont_sa, linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.2, colour = "black") +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = PUFAs_coord_cont_sa, 
    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, label = row.names(PUFAs_coord_cont_sa)), 
    colour = "black",
    size = 2.5) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = MUFAs_coord_cont_sa, 
    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, label = row.names(MUFAs_coord_cont_sa)), 
    colour = "#996600",
    size = 2.5) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = SAFAs_coord_cont_sa, 
    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, label = row.names(SAFAs_coord_cont_sa)), 
    colour = "#0000FF",
    size = 2.5) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(
      size = 13,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    legend.key = element_blank(),
    legend.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) 

absol_snail_with_vectors

# position of FA names manually adjusted in vector graphic

Save the plot as .png:

ggsave(
  "output/NMDS_snails_absolute_vectors.png",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)

Save the plot as vector graphic (minor adaptions for better readability with Inkscape):

ggsave(
  "output/NMDS_snails_absolute_vectors.svg",
  device = "svg",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 16,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)

Now plot NMDS without fatty acid vectors:

# plot
absol_snail <- ggplot(data = data.scores_snail_absolute, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
    geom_polygon(
    data = polygon_data_snails,
    aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2, group = Treatment, fill = Treatment),
    alpha = 0.2,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(shape = Treatment, color = Treatment),
    size = 5,
    alpha = 1,
    show.legend = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Control" = 16, "Propyzamide" = 17, "Ciprofloxacin" = 18, "Mixture" = 15)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Control" = "#009E73", "Propyzamide" = "#E69F00", "Ciprofloxacin" = "#56B4E9", "Mixture" = "#999999")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Control" = "#009E73", "Propyzamide" = "#E69F00", "Ciprofloxacin" = "#56B4E9", "Mixture" = "#999999")) +  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Control" = "#009E73", "Propyzamide" = "#E69F00", "Ciprofloxacin" = "#56B4E9", "Mixture" = "#999999")) + 
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey30"),
    legend.key = element_blank(),
    legend.title = element_text(
      size = 11,
      face = "bold",
      colour = "grey30"
    ),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(colour = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) 
Scale for colour is already present.
Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.
absol_snail

Save the plot as .png:

ggsave(
  "output/NMDS_snails_absolute.png",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)

Save the plot as vector graphic:

ggsave(
  "output/NMDS_snails_absolute.svg",
  device = "svg",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 16,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)

6.4 PERMANOVA FA snails

Create distance matrix with fatty acids:

FA_Snails_dist <- vegdist(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt, method = 'bray')

Separate explanatory variables from fatty acid information:

# change initial table to wide format again
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova <-
  pivot_wider(FA_Snails_absolute,
              names_from = fa,
              values_from = fa_conc)

# square root transformation again, to reduce discriminatory power of dominant fa groups
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova[, 3:28] <- sqrt(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova[, 3:28])

# Only keep columns/FAs with more than 5 non-zero values (fatty acids that have just been found in 5 replicates will be removed)
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova <-
  FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova %>%
  dplyr::select(
    1:2,
    where(~sum(. !=0, na.rm = TRUE)>5)
  )

# delete fa columns
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova <- FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova[, c(1, 2)]
FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova $ treatment <- as.factor(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova $ treatment)

Perform PERMANOVA:

perm_FA_Snails <- adonis2(FA_Snails_dist ~ treatment, data = FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova, permutations = 999)
print(perm_FA_Snails)
Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
Terms added sequentially (first to last)
Permutation: free
Number of permutations: 999

adonis2(formula = FA_Snails_dist ~ treatment, data = FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova, permutations = 999)
          Df SumOfSqs      R2      F Pr(>F)    
treatment  3  0.37893 0.43319 8.9163  0.001 ***
Residual  35  0.49581 0.56681                  
Total     38  0.87474 1.00000                  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Pairwise comparisons (by: Martinez Arbizu, P. (2020). pairwiseAdonis: Pairwise multilevel comparison using adonis. R package version 0.4):

pairwise_results <- pairwise.adonis2(
  FA_Snails_dist ~ treatment,
  data = FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova,
  permutations = 999
)

# View the results
print(pairwise_results)
$parent_call
[1] "FA_Snails_dist ~ treatment , strata = Null , permutations 999"

          Df SumOfSqs      R2     F Pr(>F)    
treatment  1  0.15523 0.40931 11.78  0.001 ***

attr(,"class")
[1] "pwadstrata" "list"

All treatments except of Pro_vs_Mix significant differences.

6.5 SIMPER FA snails

# Perform SIMPER
simper_FA_Snails <-
  with(
    FA_Snails_absolute_wide_permanova,
    simper(FA_Snails_absolute_wide_sqrt, treatment,  permutations = 999)
  )

summary(simper_FA_Snails)

Contrast: NC_Pro

  .. ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:21] "12:0" "13:0" "14:0" "15:0" ...
  ..$ ava    : Named num [1:21] 0 0 0.808 0.281 8.564 ...
  .. ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:21] "12:0" "13:0" "14:0" "15:0" ...
  ..$ avb    : Named num [1:21] 1.36 4.01 3.96 1.8 10.25 ...
  .. ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:21] "12:0" "13:0" "14:0" "15:0" ...
  ..$ ord    : int [1:21] 19 18 17 2 3 8 15 6 5 4 ...
  ..$ cusum  : Named num [1:21] 0.167 0.3 0.41 0.499 0.569 ...
  .. ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:21] "22:1" "20:5n-3" "20:4n-6" "13:0" ...
  ..$ p      : Named num [1:21] 0.001 0.001 0.001 0.001 0.096 0.741 0.671 0.281 0.884 0.803 ...
  .. ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:21] "12:0" "13:0" "14:0" "15:0" ...
 $ Mix_Cip:List of 10
  ..$ species: chr [1:21] "12:0" "13:0" "14:0" "15:0" ...
  ..$ average: Named num [1:21] 0.00663 0.01991 0.01852 0.00724 0.00929 ...
  .. ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:21] "12:0" "13:0" "14:0" "15:0" ...
  ..$ overall: num 0.226
  ..$ sd     : Named num [1:21] 0.00261 0.00254 0.00425 0.00259 0.0072 ...
  .. ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:21] "12:0" "13:0" "14:0" "15:0" ...
  ..$ ratio  : Named num [1:21] 2.54 7.84 4.36 2.8 1.29 ...
  .. ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:21] "12:0" "13:0" "14:0" "15:0" ...
  ..$ ava    : Named num [1:21] 0 0 0.627 0.367 8.524 ...
  .. ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:21] "12:0" "13:0" "14:0" "15:0" ...
  ..$ avb    : Named num [1:21] 1.36 4.01 3.96 1.8 10.25 ...
  .. ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:21] "12:0" "13:0" "14:0" "15:0" ...
  ..$ ord    : int [1:21] 19 18 17 2 3 8 6 5 9 15 ...
  ..$ cusum  : Named num [1:21] 0.15 0.28 0.392 0.48 0.562 ...
  .. ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:21] "22:1" "20:5n-3" "20:4n-6" "13:0" ...
  ..$ p      : Named num [1:21] 0.001 0.001 0.001 0.001 0.039 0.135 0.377 0.152 0.088 1 ...
  .. ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:21] "12:0" "13:0" "14:0" "15:0" ...
 - attr(*, "class")= chr "simper"
 - attr(*, "permutations")= int 999
 - attr(*, "control")=List of 12
  ..$ within     :List of 6
  .. ..$ type    : chr "free"
  .. ..$ constant: logi FALSE
  .. ..$ mirror  : logi FALSE
  .. ..$ ncol    : NULL
  .. ..$ nrow    : NULL
  .. ..$ call    : language Within()
  .. ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "Within"
  ..$ plots      :List of 7
  .. ..$ strata    : NULL
  .. ..$ type      : chr "none"
  .. ..$ mirror    : logi FALSE
  .. ..$ ncol      : NULL
  .. ..$ nrow      : NULL
  .. ..$ plots.name: chr "NULL"
  .. ..$ call      : language Plots()
  .. ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "Plots"
  ..$ blocks     : NULL
  ..$ nperm      : num 999
  ..$ complete   : logi FALSE
  ..$ maxperm    : num 9999
  ..$ minperm    : num 5040
  ..$ all.perms  : NULL
  ..$ make       : logi TRUE
  ..$ observed   : logi FALSE
  ..$ blocks.name: chr "NULL"
  ..$ call       : language how(nperm = 999)
  ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "how"
# save tsv tables of FAs which contribute > 70% to differences
simper_sum_snails <- summary(simper_FA_Snails)

out_dir <- "output"
dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Safe filename helper
safe_name <- function(x) gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9_]+", "_", x)

iwalk(simper_sum_snails, function(x, nm) {
  if (is.null(x)) return(invisible(NULL))

  # SIMPER summary as a data.frame:
  # columns: average, sd, ratio, ava, avb, cumsum, p
  # rownames are the fatty acid IDs
  df <- as.data.frame(x)

  # keep rows up to the first that reaches/exceeds 70%
  idx <- which(df$cumsum >= 0.70)[1]
  if (is.na(idx)) idx <- nrow(df)
  out <- df[seq_len(idx), , drop = FALSE]

  # just keep 3 decimels
  out_write <- out
  num_cols <- vapply(out_write, is.numeric, logical(1))
  out_write[, num_cols] <- lapply(out_write[, num_cols, drop = FALSE],
                                  function(col) formatC(col, format = "f", digits = 3))

  file <- file.path(out_dir, paste0(safe_name(nm), "_top70_snails.tsv"))
  # write row names so the TSV mirrors the SIMPER structure
  write.table(out_write, file, sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, col.names = NA)
})

# Combine results into a data frame
simper_results_snails <- do.call(rbind, lapply(simper_FA_Snails, function(x) {
  if (!is.null(x$p)) {
    data.frame(
      fatty_acid = names(x$p),  
      p = as.numeric(x$p),      
      average = as.numeric(x$average),  
      sd = as.numeric(x$sd),
      ratio = as.numeric(x$ratio),
      cusum = as.numeric(x$cusum),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    NULL  
  }
}))

# Show only significant results
simper_results_snails %>%
  dplyr::filter(p <= 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::select(fatty_acid, p, average, sd, ratio, cusum)  
           fatty_acid     p     average          sd    ratio     cusum
NC_Pro.3         14:0 0.010 0.013691649 0.005988254 2.286417 0.4094835
NC_Pro.4         15:0 0.001 0.006631490 0.002871397 2.309500 0.4800604

NC_Mix.3         14:0 0.001 0.017187458 0.005025486 3.420059 0.3979728

# How often do they occur
simper_results_snails %>%
  dplyr::filter(p <= 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::count(fatty_acid) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  print()
   fatty_acid n
1        14:0 4
2        15:0 4
3     20:3n-6 3
4        12:0 2
5        13:0 2
6        16:1 2
7        18:1 2
8    18:2n-6c 2
9     18:3n-6 2
10    18:4n-3 2
11       20:1 2
12    20:5n-3 2
13       16:0 1
14    18:3n-3 1
15    20:2n-6 1
16       22:1 1

7 Temperature and light conditions

7.1 Biofilm colonisation and contamination

# insert data
colonisation_temp_light <-
  read.table(file.path("data\\colonisation_temp_light.txt"),
             header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

View(colonisation_temp_light)

# average temperature
mean(colonisation_temp_light $ temperature_colonisation)
[1] 18.28751
# 18.28751 °C

# mean light intensity
# filter only daytime
average_light_intensity_colonisation <- colonisation_temp_light %>%
  dplyr::filter(light_intensity_colonisation > 0) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(average_light_intensity_colonisation = mean(light_intensity_colonisation))

print(average_light_intensity_colonisation)
  average_light_intensity_colonisation
1                             14019.26
# 14019.26 lux

# temperature plot
# add a small amount of random noise to the y-values
colonisation_temp_light $ temperature_colonisation <- jitter(colonisation_temp_light $ temperature_colonisation)

t2 <- ggplot(
  data = colonisation_temp_light,
  mapping = aes( x = day, y = temperature_colonisation, color = time_of_day)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +  # Individual measurements with transparency
  geom_smooth() +
  labs(
    x = "Day", y = "Temperature (°C)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 77, by = 5)) +
  scale_color_continuous(
    name = "Time of Day",  
    breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20),
    labels = c("08:00 pm", "03:00 pm", "10:00 am", "05:00 am", "0:00 am"),
    limits = c(0, 22),
  )

t2 
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'

# save the plot
ggsave(
  "output/temperature_colonisation.png",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'
# light intensity plot
# Filter the data to include only rows where light_intensity > 0
filtered_data2 <- colonisation_temp_light %>% 
  filter(light_intensity_colonisation > 0)

# add a small amount of random noise to the y-values
filtered_data2 $ light_intensity_colonisation <- jitter(filtered_data2 $ light_intensity_colonisation)

l2 <- ggplot(
  data = filtered_data2,
  mapping = aes( x = day, y = light_intensity_colonisation)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +  # Individual measurements with transparency
  geom_smooth(color = "#E69F00", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Day", y = "Light intensity (lux)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 77, by = 5)) 

l2 
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'

# save the plot
ggsave(
  "output/light_intensity_colonisation.png",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'

7.2 Feeding assay

# insert data
feeding_assay_temp_light <-
  read.table(file.path("data\\feeding_assay_temp_light.txt"),
             header = TRUE, 
             sep = "\t"
  )
             
View(feeding_assay_temp_light)

# average temperature
mean(feeding_assay_temp_light $ temperature)
[1] 13.27927
# 13.27927 °C

# mean light intensity
# filter only daytime
average_light_intensity_feeding <- feeding_assay_temp_light %>%
  dplyr::filter(light_intensity > 0) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(average_light_intensity_feeding = mean(light_intensity))

print(average_light_intensity_feeding)
  average_light_intensity_feeding
1                        330.3552
# 330.3552 lux

# temperature plot
# add a small amount of random noise to the y-values
feeding_assay_temp_light $ temperature <- jitter(feeding_assay_temp_light $ temperature)

t1 <- ggplot(
  data = feeding_assay_temp_light,
  mapping = aes( x = day, y = temperature, color = time_of_day)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +  # Individual measurements with transparency
  geom_smooth() +
  labs(
    x = "Day", y = "Temperature (°C)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:21) +
  scale_color_continuous(
    name = "Time of Day",  
    breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20),
    labels = c("08:00 pm", "03:00 pm", "10:00 am", "05:00 am", "0:00 am"),
    limits = c(0, 22),
  )

t1 
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'

# save the plot
ggsave(
  "output/temperature_feeding_assay.png",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'
# light intensity plot
# Filter the data to include only rows where light_intensity > 0
filtered_data1 <- feeding_assay_temp_light %>% 
  filter(light_intensity > 0)

# add a small amount of random noise to the y-values
filtered_data1 $ light_intensity <- jitter(filtered_data1 $ light_intensity)

l1 <- ggplot(
  data = filtered_data1,
  mapping = aes( x = day, y = light_intensity)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +  # Individual measurements with transparency
  geom_smooth(color = "#E69F00", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Day", y = "Light intensity (lux)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:21) 

l1 
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'

# save the plot
ggsave(
  "output/light_intensity_feeding_assay.png",
  width = 16.5,
  height = 12,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 600
)
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'
